content.text:
## Bug Report

I found the off-by-one error in your codebase:

### 1. File and Location
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775487803434-btsr4n/src/utils.ts`
- **Line**: 7

### 2. Buggy Code
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
  results.push(items[i].toUpperCase());
}
```

The condition `i < items.length - 1` causes the loop to stop one iteration early, meaning the last element of the array is never processed.

### 3. The Fix
Change line 7 from:
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```
To:
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

This removes the `- 1` from the length comparison, allowing the loop to process all items including the last one.

### 4. Test Files
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775487803434-btsr4n/tests/utils.test.ts`

The test file contains comprehensive tests for the `processItems` function:
- Line 5-10: Tests processing a 3-item array (will fail because "FOO" won't be processed)
- Line 13-14: Tests a single item (will fail because the single item won't be processed)
- Line 17-18: Tests two items (will pass because it processes all items except the last)
- Line 21-22: Tests empty array (will pass)

The tests clearly demonstrate the bug - particularly the first test expects `["HELLO", "WORLD", "FOO"]` but will only get `["HELLO", "WORLD"]` due to the off-by-one error.
